questions.q: In which Indian state is the Jagannath Temple located?
questions.explanation: The Jagannath Temple stands in the coastal town of Puri in the state of Odisha.

questions.q: In which town is the Jagannath Temple situated?
questions.explanation: The temple is the central landmark of the pilgrimage town of Puri on the Bay of Bengal coast.

questions.q: The temple is dedicated chiefly to which deity?
questions.explanation: Jagannath, meaning 'Lord of the Universe,' is worshipped as a form of Vishnu or Krishna.

questions.q: What does the name 'Jagannath' literally mean?
questions.explanation: 'Jagannath' combines 'jagat' (universe) and 'nath' (lord), meaning Lord of the Universe.

questions.q: Which famous annual chariot festival is associated with the Jagannath Temple?
questions.explanation: The Rath Yatra, or chariot festival, sees the deities carried out on towering wooden chariots each year.

questions.q: During the Rath Yatra, how are the deities carried through Puri?
questions.explanation: The deities ride out on three colossal wooden chariots pulled by devotees through the streets of Puri.

questions.q: The English word 'juggernaut' is derived from the name of which temple's deity?
questions.explanation: The word 'juggernaut' came from 'Jagannath,' inspired by the unstoppable chariots of the Rath Yatra.

questions.q: Jagannath is worshipped alongside two siblings. Who are they?
questions.explanation: Jagannath is enshrined with his elder brother Balabhadra and his sister Subhadra.

questions.q: Roughly in which century was the present great temple at Puri built?
questions.explanation: The present Jagannath Temple dates to the 12th century under the Eastern Ganga dynasty.

questions.q: Puri's Jagannath Temple is counted among which group of four major Hindu pilgrimage sites?
questions.explanation: Puri is one of the Char Dham, the four sacred abodes that devout Hindus aspire to visit.

questions.q: Which dynasty is credited with building the present Jagannath Temple?
questions.explanation: The temple was built by the Eastern Ganga rulers who governed Odisha in the medieval period.

questions.q: Which Eastern Ganga king is traditionally credited with beginning construction of the present temple?
questions.explanation: Anantavarman Chodaganga Deva is traditionally credited with starting the present temple's construction in the 12th century.

questions.q: To which regional school of temple architecture does the Jagannath Temple belong?
questions.explanation: The temple is a classic example of Kalinga architecture, the temple tradition of Odisha.

questions.q: What is the towering curvilinear main spire of a Kalinga temple like Jagannath called?
options: Shikhara of the Nagara fluted type only
questions.explanation: The tall curvilinear sanctuary tower in Kalinga architecture is known as the rekha deula.

questions.q: Unusually, the deities of the Jagannath Temple are made primarily of what material?
questions.explanation: The Jagannath, Balabhadra and Subhadra idols are carved from wood and ceremonially renewed.

questions.q: What is the periodic ritual called in which the wooden deities are replaced with newly carved ones?
questions.explanation: Nabakalebara, meaning 'new body,' is the rare ceremony in which the wooden idols are renewed.

questions.q: The temple complex has four gates. Which one is the main entrance facing east?
questions.explanation: The eastern main entrance is the Singha Dwara or Lion Gate, flanked by stone lions.

questions.q: The temple's enormous kitchen is famous for serving food known by what name?
questions.explanation: The temple kitchen prepares Mahaprasad, the sanctified food offered to Jagannath and shared with pilgrims.

questions.q: Where is the Mahaprasad traditionally sold and distributed within the temple complex?
questions.explanation: The Ananda Bazaar inside the complex is the marketplace where Mahaprasad is distributed to devotees.

questions.q: Atop the main spire of the Jagannath Temple stands the Nilachakra together with what other emblem?
questions.explanation: A flag is hoisted daily above the spire beside the Nilachakra wheel, a striking ritual of the temple.

questions.q: What is the wheel-shaped emblem mounted at the very top of the temple's tower called?
questions.explanation: The Nilachakra, a sacred eight-spoked metal wheel, crowns the summit of the main tower.

questions.q: Approximately how tall is the main tower of the Jagannath Temple?
questions.explanation: The towering main spire rises to roughly 65 metres, dominating the skyline of Puri.

questions.q: In the Rath Yatra, the deities travel to which temple and stay there for several days?
questions.explanation: During the Rath Yatra the deities journey to the Gundicha Temple, where they reside for about a week.

questions.q: What is the name of Jagannath's chariot in the Rath Yatra?
questions.explanation: Jagannath rides the chariot named Nandighosha, while Balabhadra and Subhadra have their own chariots.

questions.q: The main structure stands within an outer enclosing wall known by what name?
questions.explanation: The temple is surrounded by a high outer boundary wall called the Meghanada Pacheri.

questions.q: Which audience hall, used for music and dance, was added in front of the temple in later centuries?
questions.explanation: The Natamandira, a hall for ritual dance and music, was added to the temple's frontal structures.

questions.q: The classical four-part Kalinga temple plan at Puri includes the sanctum, the jagamohana, the natamandira, and which fourth hall?
questions.explanation: The Bhoga Mandapa, the hall of food offerings, completes the four aligned structures of the temple.

questions.q: A monolithic pillar standing before the Singha Dwara was relocated from which other Odisha temple?
questions.explanation: The Aruna Stambha pillar at the Lion Gate was brought to Puri from the Konark Sun Temple.

questions.q: The 16th-century Odia poet famous for his devotional 'Bhagavata' linked to Jagannath worship was who?
questions.explanation: Jagannatha Dasa, one of the Panchasakha poets, composed the Odia Bhagavata associated with Jagannath devotion.

questions.q: Which dynasty's king Prataparudra Deva decreed by temple inscription that only Jayadeva's 'Gita Govinda' be sung during Jagannath's evening worship?
questions.explanation: Prataparudra Deva of the Suryavamsi Gajapati dynasty issued a c.1500 CE inscription ordaining that only the Gita Govinda be sung in the temple's evening service.
